source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Restrict Access to the Control Plane Endpoint

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Restrict Access to the Control Plane Endpoint
input_row.description: Enable	Endpoint	Private	Access	to	restrict	access	to	the	cluster's	control	plane	to	only	an allowlist	of	authorized	IPs.
input_row.rationale: Authorized	networks	are	a	way	of	specifying	a	restricted	range	of	IP	addresses	that	are permitted	to	access	your	cluster's	control	plane.	Kubernetes	Engine	uses	both	Transport Layer	Security	(TLS)	and	authentication	to	provide	secure	access	to	your	cluster's	control plane	from	the	public	internet.	This	provides	you	the	flexibility	to	administer	your	cluster from	anywhere;	however,	you	might	want	to	further	restrict	access	to	a	set	of	IP	addresses that	you	control.	You	can	set	this	restriction	by	specifying	an	authorized	network. Restricting	access	to	an	authorized	network	can	provide	additional	security	benefits	for your	container	cluster,	including: • Better	protection	from	outsider	attacks:	Authorized	networks	provide	an	additional layer	of	security	by	limiting	external	access	to	a	specific	set	of	addresses	you designate,	such	as	those	that	originate	from	your	premises.	This	helps	protect	access to	your	cluster	in	the	case	of	a	vulnerability	in	the	cluster's	authentication	or authorization	mechanism. • Better	protection	from	insider	attacks:	Authorized	networks	help	protect	your cluster	from	accidental	leaks	of	master	certificates	from	your	company's	premises. Leaked	certificates	used	from	outside	ECS	and	outside	the	authorized	IP	ranges	(for example,	from	addresses	outside	your	company)	are	still	denied	access Impact: When	implementing	Endpoint	Private	Access,	be	careful	to	ensure	all	desired	networks	are on	the	allowlist	(whitelist)	to	prevent	inadvertently	blocking	external	access	to	your cluster's	control	plane.
input_row.audit: 258	|	P a g e Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console: 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. From	the	list	of	clusters,	click	on	the	desired	cluster	to	open	the	Details	page.	Under the	'Basic	Information'	section,	click	'Set	access	control'	link	to	view	access	control of	control	plane	endpoint.
input_row.remediation: For	exist	cluster: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. Go	to	ACK	by	visiting	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/ 2. From	the	list	of	clusters,	click	on	the	desired	cluster	to	open	the	Details	page.	Under the	'Basic	Information'	section,	click	'Set	access	control'	link	to	modify	access control	of	control	plane	endpoint. For	create	cluster: Using	Alibaba	Cloud	Console 1. When	create	cluster,	make	sure	not	checked	the	'Public	Access'	checkbox. Default	Value: By	default,	Endpoint	Private	Access	is	disabled. References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/95108.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/129425.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.6	Protect	Information	through	Access	Control	Lists Protect	all	information	stored	on	systems	with	file	system,	network	share,	claims, application,	or	database	specific	access	control	lists.	These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 259	|	P a g e

These	controls	will	enforce	the principle	that	only	authorized	individuals	should	have	access	to	the	information	based	on their	need	to	access	the	information	as	a	part	of	their	responsibilities. 259	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

The control regarding restricting access to the control plane endpoint in an Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK) can be audited using the Alibaba Cloud API and CLI tools. The description, rationale, and remediation steps suggest that control plane access settings can be configured and queried through the console and, by extension, APIs that are programmatically accessible. Checking endpoint access restrictions is a standard configuration that aligns with network rules and access control, which are typically manageable via APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ali_ack_cluster_control_plane_endpoint_private_access_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **Alibaba Cloud API Calls:** Use the Alibaba Cloud ACK API (`DescribeClusterDetail`) to fetch cluster details including network configurations that specify whether the control plane endpoint has private access enabled.
  
- **Aliyun CLI Commands:** 
  - Run the Aliyun CLI command `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetails --ClusterId <cluster_id>` to retrieve the control plane access settings for the specified cluster.
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:** 
  - Clusters across authorized Alibaba Cloud accounts and regions.
  
- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:** 
  - Check the `endpoints` section in the response for configuration information such as whether the `private_access` setting is enabled.
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** `private_access` is enabled, and appropriate IP ranges are allowlisted.
  - **Fail:** `private_access` is disabled or misconfigured, potentially leaving the control plane exposed to unauthorized access.

This approach ensures the control is verified programmatically by querying the cluster's control plane configurations via APIs, which are defined and accessible through the Alibaba Cloud platform.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can indeed be checked via Alibaba Cloud APIs and Aliyun CLI, as the configuration settings for the control plane endpoint are accessible programmatically. This aligns with the capabilities of the Alibaba Cloud SDK and CLI tools, which are designed to interact with and retrieve configuration details from Alibaba Cloud services.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The use of the `DescribeClusterDetails` API call is appropriate for retrieving cluster configuration details, including network settings related to the control plane endpoint.
- The Aliyun CLI command `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetails --ClusterId <cluster_id>` is valid and correctly specified for this purpose.
- The program name `ali_ack_cluster_control_plane_endpoint_private_access_enabled` is descriptive and follows a reasonable convention, though it could be slightly improved for clarity.
- The pass/fail criteria are clear and aligned with security best practices, focusing on the enablement of `private_access` and the configuration of allowlisted IP ranges.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- The program name could be improved to better reflect the specific security check. A suggestion would be `alicloud_ack_cluster_control_plane_private_access_check`, which emphasizes the check aspect.
- Consider including additional checks for the allowlisted IP ranges to ensure they are appropriately restricted and documented.
- It might be beneficial to mention the use of Alibaba Cloud Config for continuous compliance monitoring, as it can provide real-time alerts on configuration changes.
- Ensure that the assessment includes validation of the API response structure to confirm that the `endpoints` section and `private_access` field are correctly parsed and interpreted.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded on the capabilities of Alibaba Cloud's API and CLI tools. The technical details are accurate, and the approach aligns with standard practices for automated compliance checks.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- Modify: Update the program name to `alicloud_ack_cluster_control_plane_private_access_check` for improved clarity and consistency with naming conventions.
- Consider adding a note about using Alibaba Cloud Config for ongoing compliance monitoring, which would enhance the robustness of the compliance strategy.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the control plane endpoint access configuration for Alibaba Cloud ACK clusters is fully exposed via the Alibaba Cloud API and Aliyun CLI. The `DescribeClusterDetail` API provides all necessary details to programmatically determine if private access is enabled and if an allowlist of authorized IPs is configured. This makes the audit fully automatable without requiring manual console inspection.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
alicloud_ack_cluster_control_plane_private_access_and_ip_allowlist_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Alibaba Cloud Resource/Service: ACK (Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes) Clusters
- API/CLI Method:  
  - API: `DescribeClusterDetail`  
  - CLI: `aliyun cs DescribeClusterDetail --ClusterId <cluster_id>`
- Properties to Check:  
  - `api_server_public_access` (should be false or not enabled)  
  - `api_server_authorized_ip_ranges` (should exist and contain only authorized/allowlisted IPs)  
  - `endpoint_public_access_enabled` (should be false, if present)
- Pass Criteria:  
  - Control plane endpoint does NOT allow public access (`api_server_public_access` is false or not present)  
  - An allowlist of authorized IPs is set (`api_server_authorized_ip_ranges` is present and contains only trusted IPs)
- Fail Criteria:  
  - Control plane endpoint allows public access (`api_server_public_access` is true or `endpoint_public_access_enabled` is true)  
  - No allowlist is configured, or the allowlist contains `0.0.0.0/0` or other broad/untrusted ranges

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The only minor difference was the program name; I selected a name that is both descriptive and precise, covering both private access and allowlist checks as required by the control.
